title: Out off office Reply
excerpt: Anyone know if there is an option similar to that on teams, slack and email software that will inform another user (guest user is my particular use case) when a user in my account is on holiday.  What I have found is tha…

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Anyone know if there is an option similar to that on teams, slack and email software that will inform another user (guest user is my particular use case) when a user in my account is on holiday. What I have found is that we started using monday with clients allot, they use it 8 times out of 10 rather than emailing us, but the problem is that we have no way to let them know that we are not working today.

A few ideas:

  1. More obvious message when writing a reply that the X user is on holiday
  2. ability to add a global account auto reply in such a case

Any ideas from the community as I presume I am not the first with such a use case

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, @nealb - I like your first suggestion. Simple and easy - just let people know you are out when they begin to @mention you.

posts.author: Hingst (Sheldon Hingst)
posts.cookedHtml: We just started with Monday.com and had the same question. The minus simple is easy to overlook. Our solution is very similar to yours…take it a step further and maybe not allow a person who is out of office to be assigned or @mention - I hope this is something that can be implemented soon.

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @nealb

You do have the option within your profile under the “Working Status” tab to indicate that you are out of office and even chose the dates you will have this status for! There’s also an option to mute your notifications during this time period:

image1534×1290 141 KB

The “-” symbol will appear on the person’s profile photo to indicate that they are out of office (and any other working status). However, this will not prevent anyone from being assigned to @mentioned while they are away.

Check out this article for more details: Display Your Working Status

However, this will not provide these details to emails or messages sent via our integrations or the Emails & Activities app.

I hope this helps!

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: To be honest it not visible enough, we already use this system

posts.author: JPFisheye (Jenni Pope)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that this would be an invaluable feature to have!
Whilst you can set yourself out of office, the tiny “-” is not very visible for guest users. We have many problems with guests tagging people who are out of office, and then complaining they don’t get a response because they haven’t seen (or understood) this symbol.
Being able to send an automated response when you’ve set yourself to out of office would be a much better solution to this.

posts.author: gdonovan (Grace Donovan)
posts.cookedHtml: I advise customers to tag me directly in item updates that are in progress, which has been working great. However, I am going on vacation later this week and realize that there’s not an option for an out of office reply to these tags, as there would be if customers were emailing me. I am not worried about notifications that I might be getting, more worried that the customer will expect a response while I am out.

posts.author: byteboutiquein (Raj)
posts.cookedHtml:
We built an Out of Office Assistant for monday that handles OOO coverage automatically.

What it does

  • Each user sets their OOO dates + backup once (Global setting)

  • On boards you choose: when the owner is OOO, the app posts a clear update

  • Optional auto-reassign to the backup (Hard mode)

  • If the backup isn’t a board subscriber (private board), it won’t reassign—it will only notify in updates

  • Optional @mention auto-reply with rate-limiting

Marketplace link: Out Of Office Assistant - monday.com Marketplace

posts.author: byteboutiquein (Raj)
posts.cookedHtml:
We built Out of Office Assistant to solve it inside boards:
• Users set OOO dates + choose a backup (global)
• Admin picks board behavior: Warn or Auto-reassign to backup
• @mention auto-reply with rate limits to avoid spam
